article.title: REGENXBIO's RGX-202 Gene Therapy Meets Primary Endpoint in Pivotal Duchenne Trial as Company Eyes a 2027 Accelerated Approval
article.summary: RGX-202 hit its microdystrophin endpoint in the Phase III AFFINITY DUCHENNE trial, but Regenxbio's CEO says he will wait out the FDA leadership transition before filing.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

REGENXBIO announced on May 14, 2026 that its experimental gene therapy for Duchenne muscular dystrophy succeeded in clinical trials, [according to STAT News](https://www.statnews.com/2026/05/14/regenxbio-duchenne-gene-therapy-trial-results/). The company [reported positive topline results](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/regenxbio-announces-positive-topline-results-from-pivotal-phase-iii-affinity-duchenne-study-of-rgx-202-302771834.html) from the pivotal Phase III portion of its AFFINITY DUCHENNE study of RGX-202, a one-time gene therapy designed to deliver a microdystrophin gene to patients with the fatal neuromuscular disease.

The trial met its primary endpoint, but the company is not rushing to file with regulators. Per [BioPharma Dive](https://www.biopharmadive.com/news/regenxbio-duchenne-gene-therapy-results-fda/820145/), REGENXBIO intends to wait before submitting an application, citing the leadership transition at the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, with a potential accelerated approval now targeted for 2027.

## What We Know

### The trial result

The pivotal Phase III portion of the [Phase I/II/III AFFINITY DUCHENNE study](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/regenxbio-announces-positive-topline-results-from-pivotal-phase-iii-affinity-duchenne-study-of-rgx-202-302771834.html) enrolled 31 ambulatory boys aged 1 year and older, of whom 30 had Week 12 biopsy data available, according to REGENXBIO's release. [BioPharma Dive](https://www.biopharmadive.com/news/regenxbio-duchenne-gene-therapy-results-fda/820145/) describes the study as a single-arm trial with 30 participants receiving muscle biopsies.

The primary endpoint was achievement of more than 10 percent microdystrophin expression at Week 12. REGENXBIO said [93 percent of patients reached that threshold, with a p-value below 0.0001](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/regenxbio-announces-positive-topline-results-from-pivotal-phase-iii-affinity-duchenne-study-of-rgx-202-302771834.html); [BioPharma Dive](https://www.biopharmadive.com/news/regenxbio-duchenne-gene-therapy-results-fda/820145/) frames the same result as 28 of 30 patients hitting the 10 percent mark. Microdystrophin expression averaged [71.1 percent across all participants](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/regenxbio-announces-positive-topline-results-from-pivotal-phase-iii-affinity-duchenne-study-of-rgx-202-302771834.html), with [80 percent of patients exceeding 40 percent expression](https://allsci.com/news/clinical-trials/regenxbios-hits-phase-iii-endpoints-with-gene-therapy-for-duchenne-muscular-dystrophy/) and an average of 41.6 percent in boys over 8 years of age, a group already expected to be in decline.

Nine participants with at least one year of follow-up [demonstrated statistically significant functional improvements](https://www.biopharmadive.com/news/regenxbio-duchenne-gene-therapy-results-fda/820145/), according to BioPharma Dive. "RGX-202 is the first gene therapy in development for Duchenne to demonstrate" a statistically significant correlation between microdystrophin expression and function, said Steve Pakola, REGENXBIO's Chief Medical Officer, [in the company's release](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/regenxbio-announces-positive-topline-results-from-pivotal-phase-iii-affinity-duchenne-study-of-rgx-202-302771834.html).

### Safety

The trial reported two serious adverse events, both of which resolved. REGENXBIO described [one case of subacute myocarditis in an 8-year-old and one asymptomatic liver injury in a 10-year-old](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/regenxbio-announces-positive-topline-results-from-pivotal-phase-iii-affinity-duchenne-study-of-rgx-202-302771834.html). [BioPharma Dive](https://www.biopharmadive.com/news/regenxbio-duchenne-gene-therapy-results-fda/820145/) reported that both events were easily managed and resolved within weeks, and that average liver inflammation markers did not exceed the upper limit of normal. Common adverse events were mild or moderate and included [vomiting, fatigue, and nausea](https://allsci.com/news/clinical-trials/regenxbios-hits-phase-iii-endpoints-with-gene-therapy-for-duchenne-muscular-dystrophy/).

### Positioning against Elevidys

REGENXBIO is pitching RGX-202 as an alternative to Sarepta Therapeutics' Elevidys, which [STAT News](https://www.statnews.com/2026/05/14/regenxbio-duchenne-gene-therapy-trial-results/) reports has been hampered by safety concerns, particularly following the deaths of two recipients from liver failure. According to [BioPharma Dive](https://www.biopharmadive.com/news/regenxbio-duchenne-gene-therapy-results-fda/820145/), RGX-202 uses a different type of microscopic virus, produces a larger version of the microdystrophin protein, and is given alongside an alternate regimen of immune-suppressing drugs; Elevidys has been linked to potentially deadly liver complications and its use in the U.S. is now limited.

## What We Don't Know

The Week 12 biomarker data establishes that RGX-202 produces microdystrophin, but longer-term durability and a fuller functional picture across the trial population remain to be reported. The regulatory timeline is also uncertain by the company's own framing: REGENXBIO has tied its filing decision to an FDA leadership transition rather than a fixed date, and a 2027 accelerated approval is a target, not a commitment. The release reviewed for this article does not detail a confirmed submission date.

## Analysis

The most notable element of the announcement is not the data but the restraint around it. REGENXBIO CEO Curran Simpson told [STAT News](https://www.statnews.com/2026/05/14/regenxbio-duchenne-gene-therapy-trial-results/), "I think our data checks every single box that you would want for accelerated approval" — yet the company is holding off on filing. Simpson told [BioPharma Dive](https://www.biopharmadive.com/news/regenxbio-duchenne-gene-therapy-results-fda/820145/), "We expect that the new leadership will have a mandate on rare disease flexibility," a bet that waiting out the FDA transition will yield a more favorable review than filing immediately. For a field still absorbing the safety fallout around Elevidys, REGENXBIO is wagering that a cleaner safety narrative plus patience is worth more than speed to market.
